mRNA codon = 5’CCU3’ = proline
tRNA anticodon = 3’GGA5’
so this tRNA carries a proline
wobble says anticodon 3’GGG5’ can also pair with codon  5’CCU3’

tRNA anticodon = 3’GGG5’
mRNA codon recognized = 5’CCC3’
codon 5’CCC3’  = proline, so this tRNA also carries a proline
No problem!
mRNA codon = 5’CCU3’ = proline
tRNA anticodon = 3’GGA5’
so this tRNA carries a proline
wobble says anticodon 3’GGG5’ can also pair with codon  5’CCU3’

explains redundancy
or degeneracy in
genetic code

From the “point of view” of the tRNA:
tRNA anticodon = 3’AGU5’
complementary codon = 5’UCA3‘ = serine
so this tRNA carries a serine
wobble says anticodon 3’AGU5’ can also match codon 5’UCG3‘
codon = 5’UCG3‘ = serine

Aminoacylation of a tRNA  (“tRNA charging”)
enzyme = aminoacyl-tRNA synthetase
Step 1:
amino acid  +  ATP    aminoacyl-AMP  +  PPi
Step 2:
aminoacyl-AMP  +  tRNA    aminoacyl-tRNA  +  AMP
aminoacyl-tRNA synthetase with tRNA

Aminoacylation or tRNA “charging”
aminoacyl-AMP
aminoacyl-AMP
3’ end of tRNA
+
aminoacyl-tRNA

The exchange reaction again
Adenosine at 3’ end of tRNA gets amino acid
AMP gets an –OH for its a phosphate

“large” ribosomal subunit
“small” ribosomal subunit

prokaryotic ribosome
eukaryotic ribosome

Polycistronic mRNA – prokaryotes only!
each coding sequence has “start” and “stop”codons

(16S rRNA)
ribosome binding site
must be correct distance 5’ to AUG codon
Translation initiation - prokaryotes
fMet – used for initiator methionine only

Translation initiation - eukaryotes
initiation factors bind 7mG cap
+
normal met

ATP hydrolysis – energy for translocation
must start at 5’ 7mG cap
stops scanning at first AUG codon
stays on cap

E site - exit
P site – peptidyl-tRNA site
A site = aminoacyl-tRNA site

tRNA with initiator Met
is in P site
tRNA with next amino acid
enters A site
peptidyltransferase reaction
adds amino acid

Peptidyltransferase reaction – step 1
free carboxyl terminus
release energy

Peptidyltransferase reaction – step 2
bond carboxyl terminus to amino group of next amino acid

Elongation
peptidyltransferase reaction
large subunit translocates
small subunit translocates
kicks out “used” uncharged tRNA
next aminoacyl-tRNA arrives

Termination
stop codon in mRNA in A site
release factor binds

release factor signals large ribosomal subunit
bond between polypeptide and tRNA cleaved
protein released
ribosome dissociates
